summary: Regional anesthesia reduces pulmonary complications, postoperative delirium, and opioid use in elderly hip-fracture patients, leading to fewer adverse events, shorter hospitalizations, and improved survival—thereby decreasing both morbidity/mortality and healthcare expenses when compared to general anesthesia. This results in better overall patient outcomes and cost savings associated with regional techniques.
